name: Southwestern Creamy Chicken and Corn Soup
recipeIngredient: 4 boneless skinless chicken breast halves, cut crosswise into 1/4 inch strips
2 jars chopped green chilies (1.5 oz each)
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt margarine in a nonstick Dutch oven or large saucepan over medium heat.
2. Add onion and cook, stirring occasionally for about 2 minutes until softened.
3. Add chicken to the pot and cook, stirring occasionally for another 3 minutes until the chicken is no longer pink.
4. Cover the pot and cook for an additional 5 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through and the onions are tender.
5. Stir in flour and salt until well combined.
6. Gradually add milk, cooking and stirring over medium-high heat until thickened and bubbly.
7. Add the remaining ingredients (corn, green chilies, and peppers) to the soup and cook until everything is thoroughly heated through.
8. Serve 6 portions of approximately 2 2/3 cups each.
Per serving: 310 calories, 5 grams fat, 8% CCF, 50 mg cholesterol, 880 mg sodium
Dietary Exchanges: 2 1/2 starch or carbohydrates, 3 very lean meat
